name: Corn Chowder#1
recipeIngredient: 5 Medium potatoes, peeled and cubed (5 to 6)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook fresh corn in a covered kettle with a little water for about 12 minutes.
2. Remove the corn and add the onion and potatoes to the corn water.
3. Cook slowly until just soft.
4. Slice the corn from the cob and add it to the kettle along with the milk and butter.
5. Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Hold on a warm burner without further boiling for 30 to 60 minutes to gather flavor.
7. Five minutes before serving, add the crackers.
8. Two cans of cream-style corn may be used instead of fresh corn at other seasons; they'll want seconds.
